summary: Frontend-only validation completed for the new monthly reservation calendar on public preview URL. Monthly calendar rendering, week selection summary, booking submission flow, and mobile/desktop usability passed.

integration_issues.issue: Current public seed data had 0 full/disabled week cards at runtime, so non-selectable behavior could not be fully verified through interaction (code path exists: disabled when remaining === 0).

action_items: Add at least one fully-booked week in preview seed data so disabled/full-week UX can be validated end-to-end in runtime, not only by code inspection.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/src/App.js is significantly oversized (>700 lines) and should be split into focused modules/components to improve maintainability and reduce regression risk.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (1 runtime validation gap due to seed data)

seed_data_creation: Created one real booking via UI submission during integration test (booking_id: 18c00b44-1c51-4b0c-82f3-a32b58b42b13).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Desktop + mobile checks executed on /book. Observed 5 calendar month cards and 16 week cards. Week selection summary displayed correctly and booking submit success panel appeared. No runtime .error-class messages detected. Full-week card behavior still needs runtime validation with seeded full week.
rca of the issue: No functional defect in implemented calendar flow was reproduced. Remaining gap is data-condition related: production preview dataset currently has no week with remaining=0, so disabled-state click prevention cannot be empirically exercised. Mitigation: seed one full week and re-run UI check on [data-testid^='week-card-'][disabled].
